content.#text: Metformin is used to treat type 2 diabetes. It works by decreasing the amount of sugar that the liver makes and that the intestines absorb. Common side effects include nausea, diarrhea, and stomach pain. Metformin can cause a rare but serious condition called lactic acidosis.

Metformin is typically the first medicine prescribed for type 2 diabetes. It lowers blood sugar levels by improving the way the body handles insulin. Gastrointestinal side effects such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea are the most common adverse effects of metformin.
